key_points: Dorm rooms appear spacious with walk-in closets reported
Apartment-style housing available for upperclassmen
Limited student commentary suggests housing isn't a major complaint
key_quotes: Your dorm is so cool... I love your closet size!!!

key_points: Described explicitly as a 'bar school' by multiple students and grads
No Greek life — bar scene is the primary social outlet
Parties exist but concern raised about non-drinkers finding their place
key_quotes: Loyola is a bar school. Pm me if u have any questions as I'm a recent grad
As for social life, Loyola is a bar school but there are parties during the day
I am afraid that if I don't go out I won't have an amazing experience at Loyola

key_points: On-campus Starbucks noted positively by students and neighbors
Minimal specific dining hall feedback in available data
At least one student specifically asked for more food detail, suggesting limited info
key_quotes: Your Starbucks is a lot better than ours... it's a really nice campus
Could you talk about food specifically. Like food on campus and name the restaurants

key_points: No direct mention of counseling services in any student data
Social pressure around drinking culture could isolate non-drinkers
Bar-centric social scene may create exclusion for some students

key_points: Consistently flagged as one of the most expensive schools in the country
Merit scholarships exist and are meaningful for some students
Debt concern is real — students routinely advised to pick cheaper alternatives

key_points: Sellinger School of Business gets specific praise from alumni
No neuroscience program — students have discovered this mid-research
Jesuit liberal arts core means significant required coursework outside your major

key_points: School caught using misleading rankings in marketing emails
Claimed '#4 Best Colleges' by US News without citing the actual niche subcategory
Online student community is sparse — even students can't find Reddit presence
key_quotes: In their latest email, Loyola U Maryland wrote 'NO. 4 — 2020 U.S. NEWS & WORLD REPORT BEST COLLEGES' with no source

Googling subreddit for Loyola Maryland suggests r/loyola, though not active

Baltimore proximity is polarizing — some love it, others cite safety concerns
Located in a residential area of Baltimore, not embedded in downtown
